name: bash
description:
Execute a shell command in the session output directory.

Use for: shell operations like unzipping/archiving, downloading, data transformation (jq, awk, sort, cut), system inspection (wc, file, du), running scripts, and operations not covered by dedicated file tools. Always prefer bash over execute_code for file management, CLI commands, and shell operations.

Prefer dedicated tools for file reading, writing, editing, searching, and pattern matching — this tool is for everything else.

CWD is the session directory. All paths in commands should be relative to CWD — do NOT use absolute paths from tool results; strip any prefix up to and including the session ID. All write targets (>, >>, tee) MUST stay within CWD — writing to /tmp or any path outside the session directory will fail. For temporary files, use relative paths (e.g., ./tmp_work.txt).

Output truncated at 20 KB — for large outputs, redirect to a file and inspect afterward. Returns stdout/stderr with exit code.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Supports pipes and standard Unix tools.

description.description: Brief human-readable description of what this command does. Logged for audit, not executed.

timeout.description: Execution timeout in milliseconds. Clamped to [1000, 120000]. Defaults to 30000 (30s).
